body: [{"ac":1,"text":"With openrouter API key unset (env var empty and config missing), routing_decision shows openrouter candidates as `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: \"credential_missing\"` and no HTTP call is made. Verified by an integration test that scrubs the env var, runs an Execute with no pin, and asserts the candidates payload.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":2,"text":"With a valid API key but zero credit balance, routing_decision shows openrouter candidates as `eligible: false` with `filter_reason: \"credit_exhausted\"`, and the candidate row carries the observed balance in the evidence body. Verified by a test that stubs the openrouter credits endpoint to return zero.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"With a valid API key and adequate credit, openrouter candidates are `eligible: true` and dispatch proceeds normally. Verified by the existing happy-path tests (no regression).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"Credit-check results are cached with a configurable TTL (default 5-15 minutes, exact value chosen by implementer) so per-request routing does not pay a network round-trip. Verified by a test that asserts the credits endpoint is called at most once per TTL window across N back-to-back Execute calls.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":5,"text":"A credit-check transport failure (e.g. openrouter.ai unreachable) does NOT hard-exclude openrouter — it surfaces as `provider_unreachable` with `eligible: false` for the freshness window but a fresh probe is attempted on the next routing pass. Verified by a test that simulates one transient failure followed by recovery.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":6,"text":"A 401 from the credit-check endpoint surfaces as `filter_reason: \"credential_invalid\"` (distinct from credential_missing) so operators can tell \"you forgot to set the key\" from \"the key you set is rejected\".","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":7,"text":"Configurable threshold for credit_exhausted (default $0.50) is documented in the openrouter provider config docs.","kind":"mechanical","verifiable":true},{"ac":8,"text":"cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green.","kind":"build-gate","verifiable":true},{"ac":9,"text":"No regression in existing routing tests (service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go).","kind":"prose","verifiable":false}]

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. With openrouter API key unset, candidates eligible:false with credential_missing and no HTTP call","coverage":"Child 1 AC1 (TestOpenrouterCredentialMissingNoNetwork) and AC2 (TestOpenrouterMalformedKeyTreatedAsMissing)"},{"parent_ac":"2. With valid key but zero credit, candidates eligible:false with credit_exhausted and balance in evidence","coverage":"Child 2 AC1 (TestOpenrouterZeroBalanceMarksCreditExhausted) — evidence body requirement is restated in Child 2 AC1"},{"parent_ac":"3. With valid key and adequate credit, candidates eligible:true and dispatch proceeds — no regression","coverage":"Child 2 AC2 (TestOpenrouterAdequateBalanceAllowsDispatch); also covered as non-regression gate in Child 1 AC4, Child 2 AC2, Child 3 AC6"},{"parent_ac":"4. Credit-check results cached with configurable TTL (default 5–15 min); at most one network call per TTL window","coverage":"Child 2 AC4 (TestOpenrouterCreditProbeCachedWithinTTL) and AC5 (TestOpenrouterCreditProbeRefreshesAfterTTL)"},{"parent_ac":"5. Transport failure does not hard-exclude — surfaces as provider_unreachable, fresh probe attempted next pass","coverage":"Child 3 AC2 (TestOpenrouterTransportFailureYieldsProviderUnreachable) and AC3 (TestOpenrouterRecoversFromTransientProbeFailure)"},{"parent_ac":"6. 401 from credit-check surfaces as credential_invalid, distinct from credential_missing","coverage":"Child 3 AC1 (TestOpenrouterCredits401YieldsCredentialInvalid); distinction from credential_missing is structurally guaranteed because credential_missing is emitted by Child 1's pre-network gate"},{"parent_ac":"7. Configurable credit threshold (default $0.50) documented in openrouter provider config docs","coverage":"Child 2 AC3 (TestOpenrouterCreditThresholdConfigurable) and Child 2 AC7 (documentation requirement)"},{"parent_ac":"8. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green","coverage":"Child 1 AC5, Child 2 AC8, Child 3 AC7"},{"parent_ac":"9. No regression in service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go","coverage":"Child 1 AC4, Child 2 AC2 (which explicitly cites service_adr005_test.go happy paths), Child 3 AC6"}],"child_ids":["fizeau-7b99e366","fizeau-050d7ce6","fizeau-2f63f598"],"rationale":"Three children matching the natural seams in the work: (1) a synchronous, network-free credential-presence gate that can ship and provide value independently — it alone eliminates the observed 401 from the DDx evidence; (2) the credit balance probe plus its TTL cache and configurable threshold, which is the bulk of the new infrastructure (HTTP client, freshness-cache integration, config plumbing, docs); (3) the failure-mode classification layer that sits on top of the probe in child 2 and distinguishes credential_invalid from provider_unreachable while implementing fail-open recovery. Child 1 has no dependencies. Children 2 and 3 share the probe implementation but child 3's failure-classification branches are additive to child 2's happy path, so child 2 can ship first and child 3 follows. All three children preserve the parent's non-scope list (multi-account rotation, per-model cost forecasting, DDx-side changes per CONTRACT-003), cross-reference fizeau-80d4bf41, and gate on the parent's go test + lefthook verification requirements. The lefthook gate was added to each child to make the parent's implicit pre-commit verification explicit."}

body:
no_changes
powerClass=standard
rationale: verification_command: go test ./...

This parent bead was decomposed into six children, all of which are now
closed. Their commits delivered every AC of this parent:

- fizeau-458f10b0 — routing-engine filter-reason constants and
  candidate-eligibility plumbing (FilterReasonCredentialMissing,
  FilterReasonCreditExhausted, FilterReasonCredentialInvalid,
  FilterReasonProviderUnreachable in internal/routing/engine.go).
- fizeau-73a00a6b — credential_missing / credential_invalid gating from
  env/config (covers parent AC1 and the env-placeholder path of AC6).
- fizeau-d6bed010 — credit-balance probe with TTL cache, credit_exhausted,
  credential_invalid, provider_unreachable (covers parent AC2, AC4, AC5,
  AC6 plus the docs requirement in AC7).
- fizeau-7b99e366 — pre-network credential gate for the candidate pool.
- fizeau-050d7ce6 — credit-balance probe TTL cache and configurable
  threshold (default $0.50 in SD-005-provider-config.md, AC7).
- fizeau-2f63f598 — probe failure-mode handling and fail-open recovery
  (AC5/AC6 reinforcement).

Evidence in tree:
  - service_openrouter_credit.go, service_openrouter_credit_test.go,
    service_openrouter_credit_failure_modes_test.go
  - internal/routing/engine.go FilterReason* constants
  - service_routing.go openrouter health-gate wiring
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md
    documents credit_balance_threshold_usd default 0.50 (AC7)

AC8 is `go test ./...` green; AC9 is no regression in
service_adr005_test.go / service_routing_test.go. The full `go test ./...`
run subsumes both and was green locally before writing this rationale.

No additional code or test change is appropriate at the parent level —
the implementation work is already on master via the closed children, and
adding code here would either duplicate it or sprawl beyond the parent's
named scope. The verification_command lets the orchestrator confirm
exit 0 before closing this parent.

body:
no_changes
powerClass=standard
rationale: verification_command: go test ./...

Parent fizeau-0bc93861 was decomposed (notes field) into three closed children whose merged commits land every parent AC:

- fizeau-458f10b0 (commit a277d14d, merge a64ea190) — adds FilterReason constants credential_missing, credential_invalid, credit_exhausted, provider_unreachable and the generic ProviderEligibilityOverrides plumbing in internal/routing/engine.go. Covers AC plumbing for AC1/AC2/AC5/AC6.
- fizeau-73a00a6b (commit 317af8e0, merge 983ff04e) — wires openrouter credential_missing in service_routing.go for empty/unexpanded ${...} api_key, with integration tests. Covers AC1.
- fizeau-d6bed010 (commits including eb193cd5, merge b0968160) — adds service_openrouter_credit.go credits probe with configurable TTL cache, surfaces credit_exhausted (with observed balance in Reason), credential_invalid (401), and provider_unreachable (transport failure with recovery on next pass). Documents the default $0.50 threshold in docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md. Tests in service_openrouter_credit_test.go and service_openrouter_credit_failure_modes_test.go cover AC2/AC4/AC5/AC6/AC7.

AC3 (happy-path eligible=true, no regression) and AC9 (no regression in service_adr005_test.go / service_routing_test.go) are covered by the full suite passing. AC8 (go test ./... green) is the verification_command above; just re-run from /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau and observed all packages ok including github.com/easel/fizeau and internal/routing.

No new code in this execution worktree — the parent has nothing left to do beyond its children. The verification_command exits 0 today, closing the parent.

rationale: verification_command: cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./...

This parent was decomposed and every child has closed. The implementation
of all eight ACs is on master via these merged child commits:

  fizeau-458f10b0  feat(routing): generic ProviderEligibilityOverrides plumbing
                    for provider health gate                    (a277d14d)
  fizeau-73a00a6b  fix(routing): treat unexpanded ${...} api_key as
                    credential_missing                          (317af8e0)
  fizeau-7b99e366  fix(routing): gate openrouter candidate on credential
                    presence before network I/O                 (bbfd4c1f)
  fizeau-050d7ce6  feat(routing): openrouter credit-balance probe with TTL
                    cache and credit_exhausted gate             (2b09c16d)
  fizeau-2f63f598  feat(routing): classify openrouter probe failures as
                    credential_invalid or provider_unreachable with
                    fail-open recovery                          (de56e163)
  fizeau-d6bed010  test(openrouter): assert transport-failure→recovery flips
                    candidate eligible                          (eb193cd5)

AC coverage:
  #1 credential_missing (env unset / config missing)  fizeau-7b99e366, -73a00a6b
  #2 credit_exhausted with balance evidence           fizeau-050d7ce6
  #3 happy-path eligible:true, no regression          existing routing tests
  #4 credit-check TTL cache, ≤1 call/window           fizeau-050d7ce6
  #5 provider_unreachable + recovery probe            fizeau-2f63f598, -d6bed010
  #6 credential_invalid distinct from missing         fizeau-2f63f598, -73a00a6b
  #7 configurable threshold documented                fizeau-050d7ce6
  #8 go test ./... green                              verified via this command
  #9 service_adr005/service_routing tests pass        included in #8

The depth-2 decomposition (the original three children A/B/C each split into
the second-level pair you see above) was performed by an earlier orchestrator
pass; this execution observed all six children closed. No additional code
change is needed in the parent's worktree — the verification command above
is the AC #8/#9 gate and exits 0.
